# ReactDriver

ReactDriver is a schema-driven guided tour library for React.

Package: `@oqlet/react-driver`
Repository: `https://github.com/goutham-05/react-driver`
Website: `https://www.react-driver.com/`
npm: `https://www.npmjs.com/package/@oqlet/react-driver`
License: MIT

ReactDriver is built on `driver.js`. It adds React hooks, JSX content, TypeScript configuration, cross-route lifecycle hooks, persistence, analytics adapters, tour registration, beacons, tooltips, checklists, and testing utilities.

## Install

```bash
npm install @oqlet/react-driver
```

Import CSS once:

```ts
import "@oqlet/react-driver/driver.css";
```

Peer dependencies:

```bash
npm install react react-dom
```

## Minimal Usage

```tsx
import { useTour, type TourStep } from "@oqlet/react-driver";
import "@oqlet/react-driver/driver.css";

const steps: TourStep[] = [
  {
    target: "#create-project",
    title: "Create a project",
    content: "Start a new project from here.",
  },
  {
    target: "#invite-team",
    title: "Invite your team",
    content: "Bring collaborators into the workspace.",
  },
];

export function StartTourButton() {
  const tour = useTour({
    id: "onboarding",
    steps,
    persist: true,
  });

  return <button onClick={() => tour.start()}>Start tour</button>;
}
```

## Main Exports

```ts
import {
  TourProvider,
  TourBeacon,
  TourChecklist,
  TourTooltip,
  useTour,
  useRegisterTour,
  useTourControls,
  useIsTourActive,
  useTourStep,
  useTourSequence,
  useStepRef,
  useTourHistory,
  useTourAnalytics,
  waitForElement,
  hasSeenTour,
  skipTour,
  clearTourHistory,
  locales,
  adapters,
} from "@oqlet/react-driver";

import type {
  TourStep,
  TourConfig,
  TourControls,
  PopoverRenderProps,
  StepExitReason,
} from "@oqlet/react-driver";
```

Testing:

```ts
import { createMockTour } from "@oqlet/react-driver/testing";
```

## TourStep

`TourStep` describes one step in a tour.

- `target?: string | RefObject<Element | null>`: CSS selector or React ref for the highlighted element.
- `title?: ReactNode`: popover title.
- `content: ReactNode`: required popover body.
- `side?: "top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right"`: popover side.
- `align?: "start" | "center" | "end"`: popover alignment.
- `popoverClass?: string`: extra class for this step.
- `popoverless?: boolean`: highlight without a popover.
- `section?: string`: group label for `TourChecklist`.
- `mobileOverrides?: Partial<TourStep>`: overrides below `TourConfig.breakpoint`.
- `visibleWhen?: () => boolean`: skip the step when false.
- `delayBefore?: number`: delay before showing the popover.
- `delayAfter?: number`: delay before advancing.
- `highlightPadding?: number`: per-step overlay cutout padding.
- `canAdvance?: () => boolean | Promise<boolean>`: block Next until true.
- `autoAdvanceAfter?: number`: advance after N milliseconds.
- `advanceOn?: string`: click selector that advances the step.
- `beforeNext?: () => void | Promise<void>`: run before next navigation.
- `beforePrev?: () => void | Promise<void>`: run before previous navigation.
- `afterNext?: () => void`: run after the next step settles.
- `afterPrev?: () => void`: run after the previous step settles.

## TourConfig

`TourConfig` is passed to `useTour`.

- `steps: TourStep[]`: required tour steps.
- `id?: string`: required for persistence and registry usage.
- `persist?: boolean | "session"`: remember completed tours.
- `persistProgress?: boolean`: resume from the last reached step.
- `version?: string`: invalidate old persisted completion when changed.
- `showCount?: number`: show at most N times.
- `showAfter?: { delay?: number; visits?: number; date?: string | Date }`: schedule auto-start.
- `stepsUrl?: string`: fetch steps from an endpoint.
- `stepsTransform?: (data: unknown) => TourStep[]`: map remote steps.
- `breakpoint?: number`: mobile override breakpoint.
- `keyboard?: { enabled?: boolean; next?: string; prev?: string; close?: string }`: keyboard controls.
- `waitForIdle?: boolean | number`: start after browser idle time.
- `waitForTarget?: number`: timeout while waiting for route-created targets.
- `scrollBehavior?: "smooth" | "instant" | false`: target scroll behavior.
- `showProgress?: boolean`: show step count.
- `animate?: boolean`: animate step transitions.
- `overlayOpacity?: number`: overlay darkness from 0 to 1.
- `allowClose?: boolean`: allow Escape/overlay close.
- `renderPopover?: FC<PopoverRenderProps>`: custom popover renderer.
- `onBeforeStart?: () => boolean | Promise<boolean>`: abort start by returning false.
- `onError?: (error: Error, context: string) => void`: handle recoverable errors.
- `debug?: boolean`: log transitions.
- `onStart?: () => void`: tour started.
- `onFinish?: () => void`: tour completed.
- `onSkip?: () => void`: tour dismissed.
- `onStepChange?: (stepIndex: number) => void`: current step changed.
- `onStepEnter?: (stepIndex, meta) => void`: analytics when a step is visible.
- `onStepExit?: (stepIndex, meta) => void`: analytics when leaving a step.

## TourControls

`useTour` returns:

- `start(stepIndex?: number): void`
- `stop(): void`
- `restart(): void`
- `next(): void`
- `prev(): void`
- `moveTo(stepIndex: number): void`
- `onComplete(cb): () => void`
- `isActive: boolean`
- `currentStep: number`
- `totalSteps: number`

## Cross-Route Tours

Use `beforeNext` or `beforePrev` to navigate, then ReactDriver waits for the next target:

```tsx
const tour = useTour({
  waitForTarget: 7000,
  steps: [
    {
      target: "#product-card",
      title: "Product",
      content: "Start here.",
    },
    {
      target: "#cart-summary",
      title: "Cart",
      content: "The cart route has rendered.",
      beforeNext: () => navigate("/cart"),
    },
  ],
});
```

## Global Tours

Use `TourProvider`, `useRegisterTour`, and `useTourControls`:

```tsx
function App() {
  return (
    <TourProvider>
      <Dashboard />
      <HelpMenu />
    </TourProvider>
  );
}

function Dashboard() {
  useRegisterTour("dashboard", {
    steps: [{ target: "#stats", content: "Metrics live here." }],
  });
}

function HelpMenu() {
  const tour = useTourControls("dashboard");
  return <button onClick={() => tour?.start()}>Replay tour</button>;
}
```

## Analytics

Built-in adapters:

- `adapters.posthog(posthog, { tourId })`
- `adapters.segment(window.analytics, { tourId })`
- `adapters.mixpanel(mixpanel, { tourId })`
- `adapters.amplitude(amplitude, { tourId })`

Custom analytics:

```tsx
useTour({
  steps,
  onStart: () => analytics.track("Tour Started"),
  onFinish: () => analytics.track("Tour Completed"),
  onSkip: () => analytics.track("Tour Skipped"),
  onStepEnter: (index, { step }) => analytics.track("Tour Step Viewed", { index, step }),
  onStepExit: (index, { duration, reason }) => analytics.track("Tour Step Exited", { index, duration, reason }),
});
```

## Localization

`locales` includes button labels for:

`en`, `fr`, `es`, `de`, `pt`, `it`, `nl`, `ja`, `zh`, `ko`, `ar`, `ru`, `hi`

Usage:

```tsx
useTour({
  ...locales.fr,
  steps,
});
```

## Notes for AI Assistants

- Always import `@oqlet/react-driver/driver.css` once.
- Use `id` when using persistence, history, or global registration.
- For route transitions, put navigation in `beforeNext` or `beforePrev`.
- Use `waitForTarget` when the next target appears after navigation.
- Use `canAdvance` when the user must complete an action before continuing.
- Use `advanceOn` when a real click should move the tour forward.
- Use `renderPopover` to integrate with a design system.
- Use `createMockTour` from `@oqlet/react-driver/testing` in unit tests.
